name: DenseNet201
description: The largest DenseNet variant with 201 layers. Achieves excellent accuracy through dense feature reuse while maintaining parameter efficiency compared to older architectures.

educationalNote.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNote.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. Spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in downstream dense layers.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: All input images are resized to 224x224x3 before forward propagation.

educationalNote.detailed: Adds 3 rows/columns of zeros to borders, preparing for the large 7x7 stem convolution.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Maintains spatial representation shapes in outer boundary elements.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Ensures stem output matches Keras shape exactly.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 independent filters of size 7x7 operating over 3 input channels. No bias is used.

educationalNote.summary: Extracts basic visual patterns and downsamples spatial size.
educationalNote.detailed: Uses large 7x7 filters to capture initial visual contexts while downsampling resolution to 112x112.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Captures major shapes and boundaries while reducing computational density.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Stem convolutions reduce resolution for the dense blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.detailed: Balances data scaling across the stem network.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Avoids covariate shift early in training.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes initial features.

educationalNote.detailed: Gives non-linear shapes to features.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Permits mapping of complex shapes.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Introduces non-linearity.

educationalNote.detailed: Pads input borders to maintain visual maps across max pooling.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Protects outer boundary structures.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Pads boundaries.

educationalNote.detailed: Reduces features to a smaller grid size of 56x56.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Saves computing cost for downstream dense block processing.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling downsamples features.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.detailed: Ensures the concatenated representation is normalized before bottleneck projection.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Keeps activations scaled properly across channels.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Applies Batch Normalization first (Pre-activation ResNet style).

educationalNote.detailed: Pre-activation activation function.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Saves gradients from vanishing in deep stacks.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Pre-activation layout ensures smoother gradient flow.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Projection to 128 channels.

educationalNote.detailed: Limits the incoming channel count (64) down to a size of 128 channels, keeping computation manageable.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Bottleneck convolutions decrease parameter growth in deep networks.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Bottlenecks limit parameter growth in densely connected blocks.

educationalNote.detailed: Normalizes the bottleneck volume.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across bottleneck channels.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Applies BN prior to spatial conv.

educationalNote.detailed: Non-linear activation step.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls visual mapping scales.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Non-linear layer activation.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 3x3 spatial filter producing growth rate channels.

educationalNote.detailed: The growth rate (k) is 32. This layer only needs to output 32 channels, representing the new features discovered.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls model size while adding new visual representations.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Growth rate (k) defines how many channels each layer contributes.

educationalNote.detailed: Concatenates the block's input (64 channels) with the new 32 channels produced by the 3x3 conv, outputting 96 channels.
educationalNote.analogy: Like building a sandwich where each layer adds a new slice, keeping all previous slices intact.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Preserves features directly across all subsequent block stages.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Concatenation preserves all visual features for subsequent reuse.

educationalNote.detailed: Compresses channel volume by 50% to prevent channel build-up across dense stages, managing memory constraints.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Restores low-dimensional memory efficiency.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Compression prevents the channel count from blowing up.

educationalNote.summary: Reduces spatial size using average pooling.
educationalNote.detailed: Average pooling downsamples spatial grids by 50% to reduce resolution.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Saves compute sizes for the next block.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Average pooling maps features to lower grid coordinates.

groups.name: Input Stem
groups.description: Stem layers downsampling the high-res input image to 56x56 prior to dense block propagation.

groups.name: Dense Block 1
groups.description: Densely connected block: 6 layers with growth rate k=32. Channels grow to 256.

groups.name: Transition Block 1
groups.description: Compresses channel volume from 256 to 128 and downsamples spatial grid by 50%.

groups.name: Dense Block 2
groups.description: Densely connected block: 12 layers with growth rate k=32. Channels grow to 512.

groups.name: Transition Block 2
groups.description: Compresses channel volume from 512 to 256 and downsamples spatial grid by 50%.

groups.name: Dense Block 3
groups.description: Densely connected block: 48 layers with growth rate k=32. Channels grow to 1792.

groups.name: Transition Block 3
groups.description: Compresses channel volume from 1792 to 896 and downsamples spatial grid by 50%.

groups.name: Dense Block 4
groups.description: Densely connected block: 32 layers with growth rate k=32. Channels grow to 1920.

groups.name: Global Pooling & Classification
groups.description: Global average pooling averaging spatial channels, followed by predictions.
